sutta: [Music] the variable sariputta addressing the monk said reverences how many of these word references is a translation of the pali with sometimes that word abuso is also translated as friend referencer they replied and he said references there are these 5 ways of putting away malice whereby all malice arisen in among ought to be put away but 5 there is the case references of the person whose ways are impure indeed but not in word in such a person says all malice ought to be put away and 1 whose ways are impure inward but pure indeed and 1 whose ways are impure both in deed and word yet from time to time obtains mental clarity mental calm and 1 whose ways are likewise impure but obtains no such clarity and calm and lastly 1 whose ways appeal both in deed and word and who obtains mental clarity mental calm in each references out male is to be put away now of him whose ways are impure indeed but not in word how in him ought malice to be put away suppose a monk who robes himself in dusty rags were to see a rag in the carriage way he would hold on to it with his left foot and spread it out with his right and take and make use of the best of it and go his ways just sold sirs of 1 whose ways are impure indeed but pure inward the ways of deeds that may be impure or not at that time to be taught on let him think at that time on the ways that may be pure in this way in him ought male is to be put away and of him whose ways are impure in word but pure indeed how in him ought malice to be put away supposers a man tortured by heat but he before spent wearing craving and thirsty were to come to a pond overgrown with mossy slime and water plants he would plunge into that pond scattering with both hands the moss and plants here and there and cup his hands and drink and go his ways just sources of 1 whose ways are impure inward but pure indeed the ways of words that may be impure or not then to be taught on let him think then on the ways that may be pure in this way in him aren't malice to be put away and of him whose ways are impure both in deed and word yet from time to time obtains mental clarity mental calm how in him ought male is to be put away supposers a man tortured by heat and so forth were to come upon a puddle in a cow's footprint he might think here's a cow's footprint puddle but if i drink of it by hand or cup i shall stir and churn it up and make it unfit to drink but if crouched on all fours i were to lie and sip as a cow sips and then go my ways and he does so just sources of 1 whose ways are impure both in deed and word yet from time to time obtains mental clarity mental calm neither the ways of deeds nor the ways or words that may be in pure ought to be thought on then but the mental clarity the mental calm that he obtains from time to time let him think then just on that in this way in him odd man is to be put away and of them whose ways are like wise impure but obtains no such clarity and calm how in him ought malice to be put away supposers a sick and ailing man grievously ill were to go along the highway it might be no it might be with no village near a head or near behind unable to get proper food to get proper medicine to get proper attention to get a guide to some village boundary and suppose another man also going along the road were to see him apparently it might raise between that man raise compassion raise commiseration so that he might say to himself alas for this man he ought to have proper food proper medicine proper attention he ought to have a guide to some village why lest he suffer here wasting and destruction even just sources of 1 whose ways are impure who obtains no mental clarity mental calm in such a person verily pity ought to arise compassion ought to arise commiseration ought to arise so that he say to himself alas for this variable sir he should give up bad habits indeed and make good habits become more with our bad habits in word give our bad habits in thought and make good habits become more why lest these variables on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the wayward way the ill way the abyss hell in this way in him odd man is to be put away and of him whose ways are pure both in deed and word and who obtains mental clarity mental calm how in him ought malice to be put away supposers a man tortured by he by heed for spent wearied craving and thirsty who were to come to a pool clear sweet cool limpid a lovely resting place shaded by all manner of trees he would plunge into that pole into that pool bathe and drink and coming out would sit and lie there in the shade of the trees just source earth of 1 whose ways appear both in deed and world who from time to time obtains mental clarity mental calm the ways of deeds the ways of words that may be pure at that time letting thing on them then and the mental clarity the mental calm that he obtained from time to time let him verily think on that then in this way in him odd man is to be put away reverend says when a person comes comes to be calm throughout the mind becomes calm the releases these are the 5 ways of putting away malice whereby all male is arisen in among ought to be put away it's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is 1 of the sutas where you find the variable sariputta teaching the other monks and the buddha mentioned in 1 of 1 or 2 suitables that the remember sariputta is like his uh son just as a real turning king a universal mona has a son uh viceroy sometimes it's called that's just so what the buddha says is uh just like his son and he says that the buddha says that the verbal sarikuta turns the dhamma will perfectly and just like the buddha himself and so here we have a very good suta where the herbal sarikuta is telling the monks when you feel you will towards another monk how to get rid of the ill will and these ways basically are not to think of his bad points and to think of his good points especially in the monastery in a large monastery we need many monks to keep it running so uh sometimes uh when we live together in the monastery everyone would have their faults and if we keep looking at each other's faults then quarrels would arise and sometimes come to blows also and this has happened in some monasteries in thailand i've heard them so that is why it is very useful to forget about people's bad points and to think of the good points just like the buddha said now when you come across a dusty rag you hold it in 1 foot and stretch it out with the other foot and then you see which what part of it can be used now so whatever part can be used you take it up the rest you just head and throw it away so in this way and this this advice is also practical for lay people sometimes you work in the office with other people and you need a cooperation so they might have some faults that you find quite unbearable but in that case now you have to think of the good points and then you find that that you can uh they can still help you by a lot now uh in spite of their bad points next with times

items: of him whose ways are impure indeed but not in word
of him whose ways are impure inward but pure indeed
of him whose ways are impure both in deed and word yet from time to time obtains mental clarity mental calm
of them whose ways are like wise impure but obtains no such clarity and calm
of him whose ways are pure both in deed and word and who obtains mental clarity mental calm
